title: Kanecta Branch
description:
Per-branch metadata stored at .kanecta/branches/<encoded-name>/branch.json inside a datastore. Declares the branch's fill (full vs sparse) and what it tracks (upstream). It travels with the branch when copied or synced. See working-sets-and-branches.adoc.

The three branch classes are the valid combinations of (fill, upstream):
  - canonical main: fill=full, upstream=null  (the source of truth; at most one per lineage)
  - mirror:        fill=full, upstream set    (a complete local cache tracking canonical)
  - sparse branch: fill=sparse, upstream set  (local changes only; the rest synthesized)
Kanecta branching is independent of Git.

name.description: Branch name (e.g. 'main', 'feature/x'). The on-disk directory encodes '/' as '__'.

fill.description: full = a complete item tree (canonical or mirror); sparse = local changes only, the rest synthesized from upstream on demand.

upstream.description: What this branch tracks. null means this branch IS canonical (the source of truth). Otherwise the remote + branch it follows.

remote.description: A remote name defined in the working set's config (e.g. 'origin').

branch.description: The branch name on that remote.

branchPoint.description: Merge watermark: the per-item versions this branch forked from, keyed by item UUID. Used at merge to classify each changed item as EDIT vs CONFLICT (changed on canonical since fork).
